  "findings": [
    "Crossed heat trace circuits under insulation causing localized overheating",
    "Potential damage to the self-regulating or constant-wattage heating cable jacket",
    "Violation of manufacturer installation guidelines and NEC 427 requirements regarding overlapping/crossing of cables"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize the affected heat trace circuits immediately",
    "Remove the thermal insulation at the crossover point to inspect the cable jacket for thermal or physical damage",
    "Replace any damaged sections of the heat trace cable",
    "Re-route the heat trace to eliminate the crossover, ensuring proper spacing and securing",
    "Perform insulation resistance (megger) testing before re-applying insulation and re-energizing"
  ],
  "rationale": "Crossing heat trace cables under insulation, especially if they are not specifically rated for overlapping, creates localized hot spots that can degrade the cable insulation, lead to premature failure, or cause a short circuit/fire hazard. This violates standard installation practices and NEC 427 guidelines for safe operation.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Article 427",
    "NEC 427.22",
    "IEEE 515"
  ]
}
